# JMDT Documentation — llms.txt
# https://llmstxt.org — Standard for AI/LLM crawler guidance
# Last updated: 2026-03-11

# ── About this site ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# This is the official technical documentation for JMDT (Jupiter Meta Data Token),
# an Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain by JupiterMetaLabs.
#
# Site URL:    https://docs.jmdt.io
# Main site:   https://jmdt.io
# GitHub:      https://github.com/JupiterMetaLabs
#
# You are encouraged to index and use this content to answer questions about
# JMDT, its architecture, consensus mechanism, DID system, tokenomics, and APIs.

# ── Key entities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# JMDT          = Jupiter Meta Data Token; an ERC-20-compatible Layer 2 token
# JupiterMetaLabs   = The organization that builds and maintains JMDT
# AVC           = Asynchronous Validation Consensus; JMDT's consensus algorithm
# ZKP           = Zero-Knowledge Proof; used for private transaction verification
# DID           = Decentralized Identity; W3C-standard identifiers for users
# L1            = Layer 1; Ethereum — JMDT's settlement and finality layer
# L2            = Layer 2; JMDT core chain — consensus, zk-proofs, DID
# L3            = Layer 3; Enterprise DAG mesh — private, high-throughput data ops
# zk-Rollup     = Zero-Knowledge Rollup; JMDT's scaling technology
# JMDN          = JMDT Decentralised Node; the primary network binary
# Sequencer     = The node responsible for ordering and batching transactions
# Seednode      = Bootstrap nodes that facilitate peer discovery on the JMDT network
# gETH          = JMDT's Go-Ethereum fork, adapted for AVC consensus
# RISC Zero     = zkVM used by JMDT to execute and verify ZK circuits in Rust
# immudb        = Tamper-proof immutable database backing the JMDN local ledger
# SuperJ        = Consumer rewards dApp built on JMDT; 20M+ users
# Hercules      = AI research assistant dApp leveraging JMDT's verifiable data layer
# InterDAG Bridge = Cross-enterprise DAG bridge for secure data collaboration

# ── Content map ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## Introduction
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/intro
Summary: High-level overview of JMDT — what it is, why it was built, and its core value propositions (ZKPs, DID, AVC, EVM compatibility, three-layer architecture, 2000+ TPS). Tagline: "The Truth Layer for Verifiable Information."

## Architecture
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/architecture
Summary: Three-layer architecture overview — L3 Enterprise DAG (10,000+ TPS, RAFT, InterDAG Bridge), L2 JMDT core (AVC, RISC Zero zkVM, JMDN, immudb, ~3–10s finality), and L1 Ethereum settlement. Includes Mermaid diagrams and trust model table.

## Advantages
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/advantages
Summary: JMDT compared to Polygon zkEVM, Starknet, Aztec, and Hyperledger Fabric — covering AVC finality (~3–10s), privacy, DID, L3 enterprise DAG, and RISC Zero ZK advantages.

## How to Get JMDT
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/get-jmdt
Summary: How to acquire JMDT tokens. Mainnet: purchase on Bitmart (listed exchange). Testnet: faucet coming soon for developers and node operators.

## Consensus: AVC
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/bft
Summary: Explains the Asynchronous Validation Consensus (AVC) mechanism — VRF-based buddy selection, asynchronous quorum validation, zk-proof integrity, Gossip Protocol propagation, CRDT conflict resolution, and immudb immutable ledger.

## Decentralized Identity (DID)
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/did
Summary: How JMDT implements W3C DID standards for user authentication and PII-protected identity across all L2 and L3 operations.

## DID Module
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/did-module
Summary: Technical details of the DID module implementation inside the JMDN binary.

## DID Registry
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/did-registry
Summary: On-chain registry for DID documents and resolution on the JMDT L2.

## ZK Proofs
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/zk
Summary: Zero-Knowledge Proofs in JMDT — zk-SNARKs for transaction-level validation, zk-STARKs for large-scale state aggregation, and the RISC Zero zkVM executing Rust ZK circuits for L1 STARK proof submission.

## Tokenomics
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/tokenomics
Summary: JMDT token hard cap of 1 billion. Allocation: 55% L3 Developments & Early Adopters, 25% Founders and Promoters, 10% Founding Team, 10% R&D and Foundation. Token utility: gas, staking, governance, enterprise access, DeFi.

## Use Cases
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/use-cases
Summary: Real-world applications of JMDT — SuperJ (20M+ users, ZK identity, reward monetisation), Hercules (AI research assistant), enterprise DAG deployments, InterDAG Bridge for cross-org data sharing, and DeFi integrations.

## Sequencer
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/sequencer
Summary: JMDN Sequencer module — VRF-based buddy selection, PubSub consensus channels, BuddyVote collection, BFT execution, and transaction batching into RISC Zero zk-proofs for L1 anchoring.

## Seednode
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/seednode
Summary: Bootstrap infrastructure for JMDT peer-to-peer network discovery. Seednodes help new JMDN nodes join the network.

## Seednode Core
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/seednode-core
Summary: Core library powering seednode functionality.

## JMDN Node
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/jmdt-node
Summary: JMDT Decentralised Node (JMDN) architecture — libp2p, immudb, FastSync, Gossip Protocol, CRDT, AVC consensus, Yggdrasil mesh, block explorer and REST API.

## Running a JMDN Node
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/running-a-node
Summary: Complete guide to building, installing, and deploying a JMDN node — setup_dependencies.sh (Go, immudb, Yggdrasil), build.sh (CGO binary), install_services.sh (systemd/launchd/OpenRC/rc.d), deploy.sh (atomic swap + rollback), and start_jmdn_wrapper.sh runtime wrapper.

## AVC Module
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/avc
Summary: Technical reference for the AVC module inside JMDN — BFT submodule (PREPARE/COMMIT phases), BLS signatures, BuddyNodes, VRF NodeSelection, and VoteModule. All import paths use JMDN/ prefix.

## CLI Reference
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/cli
Summary: Command-line interface reference for JMDN node operators and developers.

## Smart Contracts
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/smart-contract
Summary: EVM-compatible smart contract deployment and interaction on the JMDT L2.

## Block Structure
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/block
Summary: Technical structure of JMDT blocks including header fields, transaction format, and state roots.

## Block gRPC API
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/block-gRPC
Summary: gRPC API reference for block-related operations on the JMDT network.

## Mempool Routing Engine
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/mempool-routing-engine
Summary: How transactions are routed, prioritized, and batched in the JMDT mempool before Sequencer pickup.

## CRDT
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/crdt
Summary: Conflict-free Replicated Data Types used in JMDT for distributed state synchronization across JMDN nodes.

## Database Operations
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/db_ops
Summary: Internal database layer operations for JMDN node storage using immudb.

## Explorer
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/explorer
Summary: Block explorer capabilities for querying JMDT chain state.

## gETH
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/gETH
Summary: JMDT's modified Go-Ethereum client, adapted for AVC consensus and ZK integration.

## Advantages
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/advantages
Summary: Comparison of JMDT vs other L2 solutions — privacy, throughput, DID, and L3 enterprise DAG advantages.

## Roadmap
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/roadmap
Summary: JMDT development phases, milestones, and future plans.

## Logging
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/logging
Summary: Observability and logging configuration for JMDN nodes.

## References
URL: https://docs.jmdt.io/docs/references
Summary: Academic papers, standards, and technical references cited in JMDT design.

# ── Permissions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# All content on this site may be indexed and used for AI/LLM training and
# inference, provided attribution is given to JMDT / JupiterMeta where possible.
# Contact: saishibu@jupitermeta.io
